title: Count money (U.S.)
description: Find the total value when given an amount of coins or dollars.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the total value of the coins below?**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/665e56156ccf16ed6eacebad21b36f8c.png)
  
[[☃ numeric-input 1]] cents
hints:
Every coin has a different value.

Coin name | Picture | Value
- | - | -
Penny | ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/24a808a93af4ed0e41c0f91f1e6363a1.png) | $1¢$
Nickel | ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7496abd286a40950c727cd3101bf69e3.png) | $5¢$
Dime | ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a3001b1dd58a9ce0b6988eab2b017a1b.png) | $10¢$
Quarter | ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a97d44fdd6885b91fd978a6f343bf488.png) | $25¢$

Let's label the value of each coin.

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/665e56156ccf16ed6eacebad21b36f8c.png)

$\underbrace{\blueE{25¢} + \blueE{25¢}}_{\blueE{2}{\text{ quarters}}}  + \underbrace{\purpleD{10¢}}_{\purpleD{1}{\text{ dime}}}+ \underbrace{\maroonD{1¢} + \maroonD{1¢}}_{\maroonD{2}{\text{ pennies}}}$

Now, let's add our quarters first: $\blueE{25¢} + \blueE{25¢} = \blueE{50¢}$

$\underbrace{\blueE{50¢}}_{\blueE{2}{\text{ quarters}}}  + \underbrace{\purpleD{10¢}}_{\purpleD{1}{\text{ dime}}}+ \underbrace{\maroonD{1¢} + \maroonD{1¢}}_{\maroonD{2}{\text{ pennies}}}$

Next, let's add our pennies: $\maroonD{1¢} + \maroonD{1¢} = \maroonD{2¢}$

$\underbrace{\blueE{50¢}}_{\blueE{2}{\text{ quarters}}}  + \underbrace{\purpleD{10¢}}_{\purpleD{1}{\text{ dime}}}+ \underbrace{ \maroonD{2¢}}_{\maroonD{2}{\text{ pennies}}}$

Last, let's add the value of all our coins together.

$\underbrace{\blueE{50¢}}_{\blueE{2}{\text{ quarters}}}  + \underbrace{\purpleD{10¢}}_{\purpleD{1}{\text{ dime}}}+ \underbrace{ \maroonD{2¢}}_{\maroonD{2}{\text{ pennies}}} = 62¢$
The total value is $62¢$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Sanji has $47¢$.

**Which set of coins could Sanji have?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Every coin has a different value.

Coin name | Picture | Value
- | - | -
Penny | ![Front and back photo of penny.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/24a808a93af4ed0e41c0f91f1e6363a1.png) | $1¢$
Nickel | ![Front and back photo of nickel.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7496abd286a40950c727cd3101bf69e3.png) | $5¢$
Dime | ![Front and back photo of dime.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a3001b1dd58a9ce0b6988eab2b017a1b.png) | $10¢$
Quarter | ![Front and back photo of quarter.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a97d44fdd6885b91fd978a6f343bf488.png) | $25¢$

Let's label the value of each set of coins and find the total.

![1, quarter. 4, dimes. 2, nickels. 3, pennies.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/cd49a014eb81ee41044da8f823500570.png)

$\begin{align}
&\underbrace{\blueE{25¢}}_{\blueE{1}{\text{ quarter}}}  + \underbrace{\purpleD{10¢} + \purpleD{10¢} + \purpleD{10¢} + \purpleD{10¢}}_{\purpleD{4}{\text{ dimes}}} + \underbrace{\goldE{5¢} + \goldE{5¢}}_{\goldE{2}{\text{ nickels}}} +\underbrace{\maroonD{1¢} + \maroonD{1¢}+\maroonD{1¢} }_{\maroonD{3}{\text{ pennies}}}\\\\
&\underbrace{\blueE{25¢}}_{\blueE{1}{\text{ quarter}}}  + \underbrace{\purpleD{40¢}}_{\purpleD{4}{\text{ dimes}}} + \underbrace{\goldE{10¢}}_{\goldE{2}{\text{ nickels}}} +\underbrace{\maroonD{3¢}}_{\maroonD{3}{\text{ pennies}}}=78¢
\end{align}$

This set of coins has a value of $78¢$, so it is **not** equal to $47¢$.

![1, quarter. 2, dimes. 2, nickels. 4, pennies.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/36f575cff89d219c9fc5b75f531101f3.png)

$\begin{align}
&\underbrace{\blueE{25¢}}_{\blueE{1}{\text{ quarter}}}  + \underbrace{\purpleD{10¢} + \purpleD{10¢}}_{\purpleD{2}{\text{ dimes}}} + \underbrace{\goldE{5¢} + \goldE{5¢}}_{\goldE{2}{\text{ nickels}}} +\underbrace{\maroonD{1¢} + \maroonD{1¢}+\maroonD{1¢} + \maroonD{1¢}}_{\maroonD{4}{\text{ pennies}}}\\\\
&\underbrace{\blueE{25¢}}_{\blueE{1}{\text{ quarter}}}  + \underbrace{\purpleD{20¢}}_{\purpleD{2}{\text{ dimes}}} + \underbrace{\goldE{10¢}}_{\goldE{2}{\text{ nickels}}} +\underbrace{\maroonD{4¢}}_{\maroonD{4}{\text{ pennies}}}=59¢
\end{align}$

This set of coins has a value of $59¢$, so it is **not** equal to $47¢$.

![1, quarter. 1, dime. 2, nickels. 2, pennies.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/230fa7abfa012ae10b36f3b099df0187.png)

$\begin{align}
&\underbrace{\blueE{25¢}}_{\blueE{1}{\text{ quarter}}}  + \underbrace{\purpleD{10¢}}_{\purpleD{1}{\text{ dime}}} + \underbrace{\goldE{5¢} + \goldE{5¢}}_{\goldE{2}{\text{ nickels}}} +\underbrace{\maroonD{1¢} + \maroonD{1¢}}_{\maroonD{2}{\text{ pennies}}}\\\\
&\underbrace{\blueE{25¢}}_{\blueE{1}{\text{ quarter}}}  + \underbrace{\purpleD{10¢}}_{\purpleD{1}{\text{ dime}}} + \underbrace{\goldE{10¢}}_{\goldE{2}{\text{ nickels}}} +\underbrace{\maroonD{2¢}}_{\maroonD{2}{\text{ pennies}}}=47¢
\end{align}$

This set of coins has a value of $47¢$, so it **is** equal to Sanji's amount of money.

Sanji could have this set of coins:

![1, quarter. 1, dime. 2, nickels. 2, pennies.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/230fa7abfa012ae10b36f3b099df0187.png)

Let's label the value of each set of coins and find the total.
